todaysPlan.activity: Homework Spelling – Silent Letters And High-Frequency Word Recognition
todaysPlan.reason: Active uploaded homework is "Benchmark Advance Spelling Unit 9 Week 3", so today's required work must anchor on Silent letter patterns (kn-, -gn, wr-), High-frequency word automaticity, Visual orthographic memory before broader profile goals.
todaysPlan.method: Read the assignment-aligned content, ask evidence-based checks, then use profile motivators only as framing.
probeSequence: Read or preview the assignment content
Ask why it happened using text evidence
Have the child explain the concept back

childProfile: Reina is an 8-year-old bilingual (English/Japanese) champion—academically a year ahead, consistently scoring 100% on spelling tests, and thriving on competition and challenge. She's a wrestler who understands takedowns and strategy, loves the Matilda movie (smart girl who doesn't back down), and celebrates wins in Japanese (すごい, やった). She has championship-level speed and accuracy when material is hard enough (beat Matilda by 700 coins in Wheel of Fortune, 10/10 flawless Speed Catcher), but disengages immediately when work feels too easy or procedural (worksheet coin counting 26% accuracy vs. store-game 86%). She self-regulates beautifully (exits on winning streaks, debugs technical glitches analytically), has a 15-20 minute focus window before needing pivot or break, and explicitly requested multiplication/division work 79 days ago—her self-advocacy deserves immediate honoring. Match her fast pace, acknowledge her intelligence, use wrestling metaphors and competitive framing, and never baby-talk or slow down. She wants to be pushed, not patronized.
stopAfter: After the active homework check is complete, or earlier if frustration is rising.
rewardPolicy: Reward only after the active homework is checked; broader review goals belong in review mode.
